forecast_read.reason: Upside Key Deal is not defensible: the buyer has been silent since 22 May 2026, the decision is explicitly gated on the SAP Ariba workshop/beta outcome, and no POC, reference calls, refreshed commercials or paper work have started. The finalist position is real, so this is partly a date and category problem, but until the beta verdict lands the deal cannot carry an upside call.
forecast_read.math: Created 6 Mar 2024 (28 months in cycle); Shortlisted since the 21 Jan 2026 onsite (about 6 months in stage); 22 days to the 31 Jul 2026 close as of 9 Jul 2026. Close date already slipped 31 Mar to 31 May (18 Mar), to 30 Jun (20 May), to 31 Jul (16 Jun 2026). Allstate itself sized implementation at roughly 9 months (21 Jan 2026), and the POC, references, commercials and the full contracting relay are all still open, so the remaining steps cannot complete by 31 Jul 2026; realistic close is Q4 2026 or later, anchored to the Ariba re-sign window around January 2027.

metrics.narrative: Pain and consolidation value are qualitatively well established from the June 2025 demo series (manual invoice-to-contract matching, homegrown supplier-risk and renewal tracking, SharePoint timesheet capture), and Allstate defined good as usability, visibility and traceability (Mohit Bothra, 25 Jun 2025). But no buyer-quantified ROI or savings target has ever been captured; the only numbers on record are Zycus-side proof points such as Tata Play's 3 to 4% ANA savings cited at the 21 Jan 2026 onsite. Until leadership hears a dollar case in Allstate's own numbers, metrics stay partial.

economic_buyer.narrative: Casey McDowell (VP - Global Enterprise Procurement) holds the Executive Sponsor contact role and joined the tail of demo#3 on 26 Jun 2025 (his words: 'very helpful'), with Cheryl Harris (Senior Vice President, Procurement) above the evaluation and Jess Merten (President, Property-Liability) also listed as Executive Sponsor. None has been engaged one to one: the Casey session has been pursued since 4 Feb 2026 (Gaurav agreed on 21 Jan 2026 to broker it) and an executive meeting with Casey and Cheryl was still being requested on 18 Feb 2026, with no such meeting logged since. Gaurav's 22 May 2026 email ('Let me connect with our leadership and take their inputs on how to proceed') confirms the decision sits with leadership Zycus has not reached.
sources: OpportunityContactRole (Casey McDowell, Jess Merten = Executive Sponsor; Cheryl Harris = Evaluator)

decision_criteria.narrative: Allstate's criteria are explicit and buyer-voiced: realistic lift-and-shift proof from an SAP stack (timelines, implementation model, real challenges: Nagendra, 21 Jan 2026), AI capability they can verify with live customers after the first reference set failed that test (Gaurav and Mohit, 21 Jan 2026), roadmap durability ('we're still probably 1.5 years away from that removal'), usability, visibility and traceability as the working definition of good (25 Jun 2025), and flexible multi-path approval workflows including flat-file lookups (21 Jan 2026). The formal RFP round scored these, and Nagendra called the RFP presentations a 'fabulous job', so the bar is known; the open question is proof, not criteria.

decision_process.narrative: The visible process ran RFP, then the three-day demo series (Jun 2025), then the Chicago onsite deep-dive (21 Jan 2026), with references and a POC as the stated proof gates and 'our executives and leadership' making the call (Gaurav, 21 Jan 2026). Allstate set a three-to-four-month decision window on 21 Jan 2026; that window passed without a decision. Since at least 21 May 2026 the operative process is an SAP Ariba workshop/beta program whose outcome leadership will use to decide how to proceed; who signs, the beta's scope, and its end date are all unknown.
sources: Clari emails from Gaurav, 21 and 22 May 2026

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW or commercial paper track has opened; the deal is still pre-decision. The one real forcing function on record is the incumbent Ariba agreement, which Allstate noted on 21 Jan 2026 re-signs with roughly 12 months remaining (around January 2027), so there is no near-term contractual cliff pressuring a fast decision. Contracting mechanics have never been discussed with the buyer.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is deeply documented across the June 2025 demos: manual contract redlining with legal check-in and check-out, homegrown systems for supplier risk and contract-renewal tracking, non-PO invoices (notably tax payments) that never tie back to a PO or contract despite a $20,000 contract-compliance threshold (David Duchon, 26 Jun 2025), and only about 70% of demand arriving planned through procurement. The 15 Jul 2025 VMS session added SharePoint-based timesheet capture outside Fieldglass. The pain is owned by the S2P operations team (Mohit Bothra, Nagendra) and framed as stack consolidation and 'it was time to reassess', not a burning platform, which is why inertia plus an incumbent re-sign is a live loss path.

champion.narrative: Gaurav (Rakesh Desai) has driven every step since late 2025: he coordinated the 21 Jan 2026 onsite, set up the POC scoping discussions (16 Mar 2026, pulling in his tech-enablement team), kept replying candidly through May ('All your points are valid... Let me connect with our leadership', 22 May 2026), and agreed to broker the Casey McDowell session. That is real champion behavior, but he holds no Opportunity Contact Role or Salesforce title, and he has now been silent across follow-ups on 8, 16 and 29 Jun 2026 (48 days as of 9 Jul 2026) on the one question that matters, the SAP beta outcome. A champion who stops answering while the incumbent runs its beta is at real risk of having lost the internal argument.

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the incumbent (P2P and invoicing, native CLM, spend reporting) inside a broader SAP estate: S/4HANA is the ERP with a migration underway, Fieldglass runs contingent labor, Concur runs T&E (Mohit Bothra, 21 Jan 2026). Allstate confirmed a formal 'SAP vs Zycus' bake-off on 15 Dec 2025, and since at least 21 May 2026 an SAP Ariba workshop/beta program has been the explicit gate on next steps, its outcome unshared despite three follow-ups. Coupa and Ivalua sit in the competitor field with no call evidence of active evaluation; Beeline was named once (15 Jul 2025) as a VMS alternative and Icertis once (25 Jun 2025) as a passing CLM question. The real fight is Zycus versus SAP incumbency plus do-nothing-and-re-sign.

critical_signals.text: Allstate's decision is parked behind a live SAP Ariba workshop and beta program, and after three follow-ups the team has shared nothing on its outcome since 22 May 2026.
The close date has slipped three times since March 2026 (now 31 Jul 2026) while the ANA proof of concept, the two AI-focused references and the updated proposal all sit undelivered.

The 21 Jan 2026 Chicago onsite won praise for the RFP showing but surfaced hard asks (realistic SAP migration proof and AI references they can believe), and the three-to-four-month decision window set that day has now passed.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A genuine SAP-versus-Zycus finalist position built over 28 months is now hostage to the incumbent's beta: Allstate went quiet on 22 May 2026 with the POC, references and updated commercials all undelivered, so the honest read is a strong competitive position losing air, not a deal in motion toward its 31 Jul 2026 date.

win_position.text: Allstate confirmed a formal SAP-versus-Zycus evaluation on 15 Dec 2025 and invested a three-day demo series (23 to 26 Jun 2025) plus a Chicago onsite (21 Jan 2026) where Nagendra called the RFP presentations a 'fabulous job'.

The full $1.5M S2P scope (iSource through eInvoicing plus Merlin and iRisk) has survived over a year of evaluation without shrinking, and pain is confirmed across contracts, non-PO invoicing and supplier risk (Jun 2025 demos).

Economic buyer Casey McDowell has never taken the one-to-one pursued since 4 Feb 2026, and champion Gaurav has not replied since 22 May 2026, so access to power is unproven exactly when SAP's beta is deciding the deal.

SAP Ariba is the incumbent across P2P, CLM and spend, holds a re-sign window around January 2027 (flagged 21 Jan 2026), and its workshop/beta program is the stated gate on Allstate's next step (26 May 2026).

deal_momentum.text: The last two-way buyer exchange was 22 May 2026 (Gaurav: 'Let me connect with our leadership'); follow-ups on 8 Jun and via the 16 and 29 Jun 2026 plans drew no reply, 48 days of buyer silence as of 9 Jul 2026.
The decision window Allstate set at the 21 Jan 2026 onsite (three to four months) expired around May 2026 with no decision, and the close date has been pushed three times since March 2026.

customer_commitment.text: The ANA POC offered on 25 Feb 2026 is still unscheduled: on 21 May 2026 Gaurav wrote that the Technology team was 'still planning for resources allocation' while SAP Ariba workshop/beta activities run.
The AI-forward references Allstate itself demanded on 21 Jan 2026 remain untaken ('we have the last reference ready to go', 7 Apr 2026), and no buyer feedback on commercials has ever been logged.

deal_risk.text: The realistic loss path is silent: the Ariba beta satisfies leadership, the re-sign lands around January 2027, and Zycus never gets its POC; three unanswered follow-ups since 8 Jun 2026 point that way.
Allstate sized a Zycus implementation at roughly nine months (21 Jan 2026), so even a positive decision cannot produce a signed deal near the 31 Jul 2026 close date.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer-voiced preference for Zycus exists in the last 60 days; the last buyer words (22 May 2026) defer everything to leadership while the SAP Ariba workshop/beta runs. The 21 Jan 2026 onsite praise (Nagendra: 'fabulous job from an RFP presentations point of view') was about the evaluation showing, not a selection, and is background by now.

business_case.evidence: Consolidation, usability and AI value are qualitatively established (Jun 2025 demos; 21 Jan 2026 onsite), but no buyer-quantified savings target, budget figure or ROI case exists anywhere in the record.

deal_movement.summary: No buyer-side movement since 22 May 2026. The visible motion is Zycus-side: a follow-up asking where the SAP beta sits (8 Jun), a third consecutive close-date push (16 Jun), and a 29 Jun plan to send the ANA SMS/WhatsApp innovation updates to force a beta-outcome answer.

items.change: Follow-up email to Gaurav asking where the SAP Ariba beta sits and whether Allstate is exploring vendors beyond Ariba; unanswered
Close date pushed from 30 Jun 2026 to 31 Jul 2026, the third consecutive push since March 2026
Plan logged to send Allstate the ANA SMS/WhatsApp innovation updates built for McAfee, to showcase innovation pace vs SAP and prompt a response on the beta outcome

day_summary.overall: The most recent day with real deal activity was 8 Jun 2026: Karson Keogh emailed Gaurav asking where the SAP Ariba beta stands and whether Allstate is exploring vendors beyond Ariba. No reply has come back; the last two-way exchange remains 22 May 2026, when Gaurav said he would take the POC and next-step questions to leadership. Everything since has been Zycus-side positioning while Allstate's evaluation sits inside the SAP beta.

items.name: Beta status follow-up to Gaurav
items.summary: Asked where the SAP Ariba beta sits and whether Allstate is exploring additional vendors outside Ariba; no reply to date.

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the dominant, active threat and the explicit gate on Allstate's decision via its ongoing workshop/beta program (confirmed still running 26 May 2026; no outcome shared since despite three follow-ups). The SAP estate deepens the gravity: S/4HANA is the ERP, Fieldglass and Concur round out the stack. Coupa and Ivalua remain listed on the account but show no active-evaluation evidence in any call or note. SAP Fieldglass (with Beeline as an alternative) is a separate VMS-only thread where Zycus is positioned as complementary, not competitive. Icertis surfaced once as a passing CLM question over a year ago with no follow-through.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: confirmed SAP VS Zycus... currently still doing a SAP Ariba workshop/beta program... no update on the beta or evaluation from gaurav
competitors.how_we_win: Differentiate on Merlin/ANA AI maturity, faster non-SAP-centric integration, and deliver the overdue AI-scale references and ANA POC to prove the roadmap story before the beta concludes and before the Ariba renewal window (roughly Jan 2027) closes.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.source: Opportunity Competitors__c field (no corroborating call evidence)
competitors.how_we_win: No active evaluation evidence; if it resurfaces, lead with single-suite integration depth and Merlin AI maturity vs Coupa's point-in-time scale narrative.

competitors.name: Ivalua
competitors.source: Opportunity Competitors__c field (no corroborating call evidence)
competitors.how_we_win: No active evaluation evidence; if it resurfaces, lead with faster deployment and agentic AI (ANA) vs Ivalua's low-code flexibility pitch.

competitors.name: SAP Fieldglass
competitors.quote: Fieldglass is is our current application, which we use in order to cater the needs for the contingent workforce and the statement of work perspective
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as complementary via integration for PO/invoice/contract-consumption tracking; explicitly do not attempt to displace Fieldglass for contingent-labor management.

competitors.name: Beeline
competitors.quote: whether it's a Fieldglass, beeline, or you choose to stick to Fieldglass or you go to an alternative VMS solution
competitors.how_we_win: Not a live thread; no action needed unless Allstate reopens the VMS-replacement question.

competitors.quote: Are you leveraging the native CLM, or have you also been transferred to the iCertis module team? ... Native CLM.
competitors.how_we_win: Single passing question over a year ago; Allstate confirmed it runs Ariba's native CLM. Reinforce AI-powered redlining/CLM if it resurfaces.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Allstate's evaluation criteria were set across the Jun 2025 demos and the 21 Jan 2026 onsite and have not been refreshed since (no buyer session in the last 90 days). Zycus is aligned on usability and workflow flexibility, partially aligned on SAP lift-and-shift proof, and exposed on AI proof at scale, which is exactly the axis the SAP Ariba beta is testing.

best_practice_check.summary: The deal is single-threaded through a quiet champion while the incumbent runs a beta that is scoring exactly the criterion (AI proof) Zycus has not yet evidenced. The levers are a second thread to power, proof before the beta concludes, and a buyer-owned timeline anchored to the real forcing function.
flags: Break the single thread: the economic buyer Casey McDowell has never met one to one despite five months of asks; make the executive session the deal's next gate, framed on the January 2027 Ariba renewal decision.
Fight the beta with proof, not follow-ups: the two AI-scale references and the ANA POC with agreed success criteria are the direct counter to the SAP Ariba beta; land them before it concludes.
Replace slipping monthly close dates with a mutual evaluation plan anchored to the Ariba re-sign window; the 31 Jul 2026 date has no buyer-agreed steps behind it.

champion_strength.summary: Gaurav (Rakesh Desai) has been the primary internal driver since late 2025, coordinating the January 2026 onsite, brokering the ANA POC discussions, and relaying leadership steps, but he has gone silent on the single most decision-relevant question (the SAP Ariba beta outcome) across three consecutive follow-ups since 22 May 2026 (48 days as of 9 Jul 2026), raising real risk that he has lost internal traction or is waiting out the incumbent's beta before re-engaging.

scope_change.detail: Full S2P scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin, iRisk) unchanged since the June 2025 evaluation. Contingent-workforce (VMS) was explicitly scoped out on 15 Jul 2025, with Zycus positioned to integrate with SAP Fieldglass rather than replace it.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is the explicit differentiation wedge: Merlin intake, ANA autonomous negotiation and the agentic roadmap led the 21 Jan 2026 onsite, and the 29 Jun 2026 plan sends fresh ANA innovation proof to contrast with SAP's pace. The exposure is proof, not positioning: Allstate's buyer-voiced doubt (21 Jan 2026) that references could not evidence AI at scale is only half-answered while the reference calls and POC remain undone.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Allstate is AI-forward: Mohit Bothra leads a dedicated AI and automation stream for procurement, the team probed agent-building, early-adopter programs and AI governance at the 21 Jan 2026 onsite, and their evaluation explicitly scores vendors' AI credibility, with references demanded specifically on AI adoption.

items.detail: Incumbent SAP Ariba workshop/beta program is the stated gate on the decision; its scope and end date are unknown and Allstate has shared nothing on its outcome since 22 May 2026.
Executive session with Casey McDowell (and Cheryl Harris) pursued since 4 Feb 2026 has never been held; the economic buyer has not been engaged one to one in the entire cycle.
Single-threaded through Gaurav, who has not replied to three follow-ups since 8 Jun 2026; if he has lost internal traction there is no second live buyer thread.

AI-credibility gap from the original reference set (buyer could not 'get a good sense of their AI use cases'); the two replacement AI-forward references are lined up but the calls have never been completed.

The decision window set on 21 Jan 2026 (three to four months) has passed; the close date has slipped 31 Mar to 31 May to 30 Jun to 31 Jul 2026, and the true forcing function is the Ariba re-sign around January 2027.

confidence_signals.summary: The deal is cooling: buyer reply cadence collapsed after 22 May 2026, the decision window Allstate set in January has passed, and every proof gate (POC, references, commercials) remains open while the incumbent's beta runs.
items: Last two-way buyer exchange 22 May 2026 ('give me some time'); follow-ups on 8 Jun and plans on 16 and 29 Jun 2026 drew no response (48 days silent as of 9 Jul 2026).
Close date pushed three times since March 2026 (31 Mar to 31 May to 30 Jun to 31 Jul).
ANA POC unscheduled more than four months after the 25 Feb 2026 offer; final reference 'ready to go' since 7 Apr 2026 but never taken.

ceo_intervention.summary: $1.5M Allstate deal quiet 25 days; champion cold on SAP beta outcome

reasons.detail: Allstate ($1.5M, Upside Key Deal, Shortlisted, close 7/31) has had zero logged activity for 25 days and momentum is only 52. Karson's 6/29 note says he is 'hoping' a McAfee innovation share 'generates a response one way or another,' after 6/16 recorded 'no update on the beta or evaluation from Gaurav' - the champion has been silent since 22 May and the award is gated on a live SAP Ariba beta we cannot see into. If this keeps drifting, the incumbent Ariba renewal wins by default and a $1.5M forecasted deal slips out of the quarter.

contributions.detail: Economic buyer never engaged — the Casey McDowell exec session has been pursued since 4 Feb 2026 and still hasn't happened, leaving EB outreach entirely indirect via Gaurav. ► Force the exec session before the SAP beta outcome lands.
SAP Ariba explicitly gates the decision — Allstate confirmed still running the SAP Ariba workshop/beta program as of 26 May 2026, with no outcome shared despite three follow-ups since.
Single-threaded and stalling — champion Gaurav has not responded across the 6.8, 6.16, 6.29.26 next-step entries; no second live buyer thread exists.

Deep historical qualification — decision criteria and pain are confirmed, competition is well-mapped, and five recorded/transcribed product sessions (Jun 2025–Jan 2026) built real technical alignment.
Reference credibility gap — the original reference set left Allstate unable to "get a good sense of their AI use cases"; the two replacement AI-forward references remain unconfirmed with the buyer.

Stage-reality check: recorded Shortlisted (cap 60), but 169 days dark, EB never met, and four close-date slips (3/31→7/31/26) indicate the deal is behind its recorded position, not at it — scored well below the Shortlisted anchor. ► Re-validate stage/forecast category in SFDC to reflect current reality.
No buyer meeting in 169 days — last recorded session was the 21 Jan 2026 onsite; zero meetings logged in the 30/60/90-day windows.

Champion silent on the one question that matters — three consecutive follow-ups (6.8.26, 6.16.26, 6.29.26) asking about the SAP Ariba beta outcome logged with "no update from gaurav." ► Escalate past Gaurav to a second Allstate stakeholder.
Close date slipping repeatedly — pushed 3/31 → 5/31 → 6/30 → 7/31/2026 with no new anchor event behind the latest date. ► Tie any new close date to a concrete buyer milestone, not a re-forecast.
Entire Feb–June 2026 trail is rep-only outreach — invites, videos, and FU asks from KK/Alexa with no confirmed buyer reply logged anywhere in that window.
Rep cadence still active — a concrete, dated 6.29.26 action (Alexa sharing ANA/SMS innovation proof with Gaurav) keeps a live thread open even without response.
No future meeting on the calendar — next_scheduled is null; nothing dated to force re-engagement.
Paper process shows a gap — MEDDPICC marks paper_process as "gap," with no contracting or commercial motion evidenced.
Buyer's real investment sits with SAP, not us — Allstate is actively running its own SAP Ariba workshop/beta while Zycus asks (POC, commercials, references) go unanswered since April 2026.
No exec-level buyer action — the Casey McDowell/Cheryl Harris exec session requested since Feb 2026 never materialized.
Historical buyer-side engagement was real — Allstate hosted a 92-minute onsite (21 Jan 2026) and an AI/automation-specific track led by Mohit Bothra, plus five transcribed demos in 2025.
No buyer-initiated action item since January — Gaurav's only proactive claim (5.26.26, "connecting with leadership on next steps") produced no confirmed follow-through.
Active competitor gating the award — SAP Ariba's live beta/workshop is the explicit gate on Allstate's decision, confirmed running as of 26 May 2026 with no outcome since.
Economic buyer inaccessible — Casey McDowell has had zero direct Zycus engagement despite five months of pursuit (since 4 Feb 2026).
Single point of failure on champion — Gaurav is the only buyer thread and has gone dark across three consecutive follow-ups (6.8–6.29.26).
Close date repeatedly pushed — four slips (3/31→5/31→6/30→7/31/2026), only 22 days to close, no confirmed new catalyst.
169 days without a buyer meeting — last recorded session 21 Jan 2026; zero meetings in the 30/60/90-day windows.
Paper process gap plus soft forecast — no commercial motion evidenced and the deal still carries "Upside Key Deal," not Commit.

win_position.text: Economic buyer never engaged — the Casey McDowell exec session has been pursued since 4 Feb 2026 and still hasn't happened, leaving EB outreach entirely indirect via Gaurav. ► Force the exec session before the SAP beta outcome lands.

Single-threaded and stalling — champion Gaurav has not responded across the 6.8, 6.16, 6.29.26 next-step entries; no second live buyer thread exists.

Deep historical qualification — decision criteria and pain are confirmed, competition is well-mapped, and five recorded/transcribed product sessions (Jun 2025–Jan 2026) built real technical alignment.
Reference credibility gap — the original reference set left Allstate unable to "get a good sense of their AI use cases"; the two replacement AI-forward references remain unconfirmed with the buyer.

Stage-reality check: recorded Shortlisted (cap 60), but 169 days dark, EB never met, and four close-date slips (3/31→7/31/26) indicate the deal is behind its recorded position, not at it — scored well below the Shortlisted anchor. ► Re-validate stage/forecast category in SFDC to reflect current reality.

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: SAP Ariba is the dominant, active threat and the explicit gate on Allstate's decision via its ongoing workshop/beta program (confirmed still running 26 May 2026; no outcome shared since despite three follow-ups).

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Call Mohit Bothra directly for the Ariba beta status and to restart ANA POC scheduling. (by 17 Jun)
Send Gaurav the SAP migration readiness checklist and data templates promised at the January onsite. (by 23 Jun)
Book the Casey McDowell and Cheryl Harris executive session framed on the January 2027 Ariba renewal decision. (by 7 Jul)
